metadata.title:
COUNCIL AT LAST


IS THINKING ABOUT
HOWARD ST. BRIDGE
2 ~ ~ ~s~@ ~ -

pages.raw_text:
Any repairs have been pronounc-
ed by the city engineer as made
quate.


Then the water power company
comes to the city council with it's
tale of woe and asks the city to
assist in building a new bridge, and
the city council at last nights
meeting, in addition to the forego-
ing resolution, set aside $4,000 for
this bridge in this years budget.


Now, why should the city help
build this bridge? The city isn't
interested in this franchise. The
people of the North Side are not
demanding it. The present bridge
is suitable for present traffic and
all traffic except street car. Then
why is the city exerting itself to
raise this bridge money?


What will happen if they don't
vote the money for this bridge?
The Washington Water Power com
pany will build that bridge itself
or it won't run street cars under
that franchise. If they don't run
